name: Monstera aureopinnata
aboutText: Monstera aureopinnata is a stunning climbing species native to the humid lowlands and premontane habitats of Costa Rica and Panama. It ascends tree trunks using aerial roots, thriving in warm, moist conditions with filtered light beneath the forest canopy. This plant is characterized by deeply pinnatid adult leaves with numerous narrow lobes and exhibits heteroblastic growth, transitioning from entire juvenile leaves to highly dissected adult forms.

morphology.venation: Central midrib with prominent lateral veins

fieldNotes.title: Pinnate and Properly Scarce
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: A genuinely scarce Monstera from Costa Rica and Panama that produces, in maturity, pinnate leaves — properly divided into separate leaflets rather than merely fenestrated, a growth form unusual enough to make a mature specimen look like a different genus entirely. Ultra rare in cultivation with appropriately serious pricing. The juvenile form gives little indication of what is coming; the adult form is, for a Monstera, extraordinary. If you can secure a rooted cutting at any point, do so promptly and grow it on with patience. The transformation from juvenile to adult is the event you are working toward.
